body.u: You know I was never very clever at school. Being that, people took the micky but I took knowno notice of them. people, when we did a test laughftlaughed at me when I got near bottom marks.

I remember like it was yesterday, I went for an interview 3 mounthmonth or, there about’sabout before I was due to leave school This was for the merchant Navy but failed the entry test. it was quitquite a blow for me I felt rejected yet again.
My dada recondreckoned that he could get me a job at blueits but there were no vacancies as yet.
I left school and on to the dole with thousands of other people.

I didntdidn't want to but in our small town of QueenboughQueenborough more of a village really people talk about me and how I was on the dole they thought it was discracefuldisgraceful but what else could a reajectreject do as a result I spent a lot of time on the key side at sheerness watching the trottrout boat leave every hour to travel out to a large lash ship to see if there is anything needed like beer or a sick person needing to be taken ashore the people all knew me, and they I was on the dole but they didn’t mock me one morning one of the men Joe said there was a vancancievacancy for this job and he would put in a good word for me
I got the job not a bad wage and I was happy at my work at 25 I had almost became a permenetpermanent fixture sometime’ssometimes I slept there.

I did trip after trip after trip I brought back the sick people abroardaboard the people who beforbeforelaughtlaughed at me at the age of 35 I had moved in to the boat and was known as old Killer the boat man I rescued people who almastalmost drowned and ernedearned quite a respect from other people I put a bit by each week for my retiar mean’tretirement and a boat of my own to live on.
I never got married although with one girl I was pritypretty serious but she died in an accident just my luck in fact it was typical of my luck,

2 years ago I retiardretired the firm instead of a gold watch gave me the old boat they said they were getting a new one now hearhere in the thamesThames I spend my time and from time to time am asked to do trip’s in my boat to the lash when other people were ill. I am respected by all. if the people who mocked me all those years ago could see me now My mother and father died long ago I was settsent back alonga long way when they died I buried them at sea with all the trimmings I was 41 then and Now ImI'm 68 a gabberinggibbering old man who is a respected old pest.
